He feels pity for her

questions.question: What is a theme of the excerpt?

responses.content: C. Gifts are given to reward the completion of complicated tasks.

questions.question: Which sentence from the excerpt most strongly supports the correct answer to the previous question?

responses.content: B. “It is better to die like a hero than to live like an ox in a stall.”

questions.question: Reread paragraph 13. Why do cell phones display “NO SIGNAL” after everyone lands in Rhode Island?

responses.content: Because people transported together in one place
They magically got transported

questions.question: According to the author, how would a coordinated jump affect the Earth's orbit?

responses.content: Because the cell networks have all collapsed under the unprecedented load

questions.question: Reread paragraphs 19-20. What do these paragraphs reveal about the impact humans have on Earth compared to the impact they have on each other?

responses.content: It does not affect the planet. Earth outweighs us by a factor of over ten trillion

questions.question: What is the central idea of “Everybody Jump (from What If?)?”

responses.content: A. When people work together, they can accomplish amazing physical feats like moving a planet.

questions.question: Which sentence from the text best supports the correct answer to the previous question?

responses.content: A. “Our species staggers on, but our population has been greatly reduced. Earth's orbit is completely unaffected—it spins along exactly as it did before our species-wide jump.”

responses.content: D. Margaret is a great example for women because she built a successful career in a mostly male profession.
